name: Quaernan
description: You found this ship when it crashed, derelict, into the side of Hope, punching a crater so deep it nearly broke through the planet's crust. The ship is far beyond the size of any ship humanity has even contemplated building before, able to house enough people to populate a dozen worlds and able to carry the cargo capacity of many dozens of bulk freighters. Despite the fact that all facets of its engineering seem to be beyond humanity's capacities, many of the systems inside closely resemble human technology.

name: Alpaca
description: This appears to be a heavily modified and upgraded Mule, with all human aspects stripped out in favour of thicker armor and remote or AI controls. The inside is filled almost entirely with tech and metal, save for cramped, uncomfortable access shafts. The systems seem to be self-maintaining, however, so the use of the ducts is questionable at best. Surprisingly for a ship of its size, this ship seems to have no hyperdrive whatsoever; when it's time to jump, these ships will dock inside the Quaernan.

name: Llama
description: Unlike the Alpaca, this is a functional, liveable ship with the amenities needed to sustain a significant human populous, while also being a capable combat ship in its own right. Exceedingly durable and armed with a good mix of precision and area-effect weaponry, it makes an excellent ship all on its own. Amazingly, the Quaernan has bay space reserved for thirty five of these ships, lending it some significant extra firepower.

name: Casparna
description: Compared to the smaller Alpacas and Llamas, the Casparnans feel like complete black sheep. They're marginally slower, with less focus on range and more focus on direct firepower and powerful regeneration units. These ships are insanely hard to kill, especially in herds.

name: Lianba
description: These enormous vessels are autonomous freighter-transport hybrids, designed not for engaging enemies, but resisting their fire. Despite the fact that these ships would do best with hyperdrives, they operate seemingly as support units for smaller ships like the Alpacas or Llamas, carrying large amounts of supplies or reserve crew within the systems they patrolled. 	When all is said and done, however, they can be packed up into the Quaernan for interstellar travel. With the Quaernan's already huge cargo capacity, one is left to wonder just what would be carried that required additional dedicated ships.
